steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes what the researchers did to pots of already-sterilized (microbe-free) soil. The phrase that follows — "with slurries of live microbes" — tells us they were introducing living organisms into an empty environment. The word must mean something like "fill with inhabitants."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — sanitized: means to clean or sterilize — but the soil is already sterilized. This repeats what was already done. ✗
  • Choice B — populated: means to fill with inhabitants or living things — exactly what adding microbial slurries to empty soil accomplishes. ✓
  • Choice C — precluded: means to prevent or rule out — the opposite of adding microbes. ✗
  • Choice D — estimated: means to approximate a value — unrelated to the physical act of adding microbes. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
